reasoning: The baseline value on 2026-03-01 corresponds to the data released on 2026-02-26 for the week of 2026-02-25, which is 3.0. A resolution of YES requires the value to be strictly greater than 3.0. Looking at the weekly FRED data over the past 4 months, repo facility usage follows a distinct cyclical pattern: a brief sequence of large spikes typically gives way to a 2–3 week 'lull' period where usage stays at minimal levels (0.0 to 3.0). We are currently entering a lull; values just dropped from a peak of 3,001 (Feb 4) and 113 (Feb 11) to 6.0 (Feb 18) and exactly 3.0 (Feb 25). Following previous patterns, the next week (March 4, corresponding to the March 8 resolution) is highly likely to continue the lull with values of 0, 1, or 2, making an increase (value > 3.0) unlikely. Hence, the probability for March 8 is set at 0.35. For the March 31 resolution (covering the March 25 data), this is exactly four weeks out; historically, this is enough time for the cycle to revert to a spike or for random liquidity needs to emerge, bringing the probability much closer to the historical base rate of ~0.50. I assign 0.48 to appropriately increase uncertainty over the longer horizon.
